steps.title: Identify the exponential growth model
steps.content: This is exponential growth where the population multiplies by the same factor each period. The general form is $y = a \cdot b^t$, where $a$ is the initial amount and $b$ is the growth factor per time period. Here, $a = 48,000$ (initial bacteria) and $b = 2$ (because doubles every day means multiply by 2).

steps.title: Apply the formula and verify
steps.content:
Substitute into the exponential growth formula:
$$y = 48,000(2)^t$$
Test at $t = 1$: $y = 48,000(2)^1 = 96,000$, which shows the population doubled from the initial 48,000. That matches choice D — D is correct.
